of LEDDisplay Technologies
1. Surface Mounted Device (SMD) LED Displays. SMD LED displays use surface-mounted parts to create an efficient viewing experience. Since the displays are mounted directly on a circuit board rather than through a hole, SMD LED displays are more space efficient, unlike conventional LED displays.
2. Chip-On-Board (COB) LED Displays. Chip-On-Board (COB) mounts LED chips on a printed circuit board (PCB) to create a high-intensity lighting solution. Placing the chips directly onto the PCB creates a higher chip density and a flat, uniform LED surface.
3. Direct-View LED Displays. Unlike the other LED displays, the direct-view LED display requires no encapsulation layer to display high brightness and contrast.
4. Transparent LED Displays. This LED display technology has a transparent design that allows you to see through the screen. It’s like a two-way mirror, only that you get to see the visuals on both sides of the screen.
5. Flexible LED Displays. When considering flexible LED displays, ponder the concept of versatility. You can curve, bend, and shape the displays with this technology in any way you choose.
